reviews.review_text:
Honestly a game changer for desktop PCs, having an animated and interactive desktop makes things so much better. You can have your wallpaper react to sounds like music, react to you moving your mouse, and other cool stuff like that.

I also find it hilarious how people in the reviews go on rant about nsfw content. Keep in mind that the only way to see this content is to go out of your way to enable it in the apl's settings, which means all these people complaining about seeing titties went out of their way to enable that setting just to complain about it
If you can sift through the mounds of anime girls theres some pretty cool nature and video game themed wallpapers. The community makes some great stuff (when they're not horny).

I really recommend this app it is very clean and easy to use especially for old people, also easy to access and versatile even though it costs money but very pleased with this purchase and highly recommended it is easy in the top 5 apps of all time.

This thing was awesome for about 6 months when I first got it. Now? The engine constantly  crashes, goes unresponsive, or straight up just doesn't even load the wallpaper in. After a reinstall, it got even worse. Now its CTD every time I try to load up a wallpaper. I have to use task manager to force restart, and it just does it all over again. What happened?

Writing this negative review because it is 2026 and we still don't have proper, native Linux support. The developers have been dodging this for years, hiding behind the same old excuses, but let’s be real—the current ecosystem completely allows for it.

With modern Wayland protocols, robust graphics pipelines, and the tools available today, bringing Wallpaper Engine to Linux is entirely doable. The technical limitations they used to complain about simply don’t exist anymore. At this point, it’s not a matter of capability; it’s pure laziness from the dev team.

I honestly believe they are just comfortable collecting money from the massive Windows user base while completely ignoring the growing Linux and Steam Deck community that wants native desktop integration, not just a mobile companion app.

I’ll gladly flip this to a positive review the second the developers stop being lazy and give us proper Wayland support. Until then, it's a hard pass.

English :
Honestly, Wallpaper Engine is a great piece of software if you like customizing your PC. There are tons of animated wallpapers (sometimes too many '^^) thanks to the Steam community, so you can easily find something that suits your taste, whether it's gaming, anime, landscapes, or anything else—there's something for everyone.

The software is easy to use and doesn't consume many resources in most cases. Plus, the wallpapers pause when you launch a game in fullscreen mode, which prevents performance issues.

The only minor drawback is that there are so many choices that you can sometimes spend a lot of time searching for the perfect wallpaper (as evidenced by the amount of time I spent on it...). Some user-created wallpapers are also better made than others.

But for the price, it's definitely worth it. If you want to give your desktop a more vibrant look without any hassle, I highly recommend Wallpaper Engine.

----------------------------------------------------------------------

Français :
Franchement, Wallpaper Engine est un super logiciel si vous aimez personnaliser votre PC. Il y a énormément de fonds d'écran animés (parfois trop '^^ ) grâce à la communauté Steam, donc on trouve facilement quelque chose qui correspond à ses goûts, que ce soit du gaming, de l'anime, des paysages ou n'importe quoi d'autre, il y a de tout.

Le logiciel est simple à utiliser et ne consomme pas beaucoup de ressources dans la plupart des cas. En plus les fonds d'écran se mettent en pause quand on lance un jeu en plein écran, ce qui évite de perdre des performances.

Le seul petit défaut, c'est qu'il y a tellement de choix qu'on peut parfois passer du temps à chercher le fond d'écran parfait ( comme l'indique mon temps passer dessus... ). Certains fonds d'écran créés par les utilisateurs sont aussi mieux réalisés que d'autres.

Mais pour le prix, ça vaut largement le coup. Si vous voulez donner un style plus vivant à votre bureau sans vous prendre la tête, je recommande clairement Wallpaper Engine.

The Best $4 You Will Ever Spend on Steam
Wallpaper Engine is not a game, but it is an absolute mandatory download for anyone with a PC. It transforms your boring, static desktop into a living, breathing canvas of interactive art, music-responsive visuals, and stunning animations.

PROS:
Infinite Content: The Steam Workshop integration gives you free access to millions of community-made wallpapers (Anime, Cyberpunk, Nature, Minimalist, Games, etc.).

Ultra-Optimized: It barely touches your system resources. It automatically pauses the wallpaper when you launch a game or maximize a window, ensuring zero FPS loss.

Insane Customization: You can change playback speeds, adjust colors, toggle audio elements, and even sync the lighting with your Corsair or Razer RGB setups.

No Subscriptions: You buy it once for the price of a coffee, and you own it forever with no ads or hidden microtransactions.

CONS:
Spending 3 hours browsing wallpapers instead of actually playing games.

Verdict:
A flawless utility app that brings life to your desktop setup. Once you use it, looking at a normal, static background feels completely empty.
10/10 - A Masterpiece for Your Monitor.
Best app for the buck, I can now trigger my epilepsy by just looking at my monitor moving while on on windows desktop.

This is it, the wallpaper app to be used for generations (or at least till a Microslop change renders it unusable).

Wallpaper Engine:

Is a must-have tool for every PC.

This is not really a game, but it is easily one of the best things you can buy on Steam. It lets you use animated, interactive, audio-responsive, and custom wallpapers, and the Steam Workshop support makes it even better. There are thousands of wallpapers available, so you can make your desktop look clean, cozy, dark, anime-themed, gaming-themed, minimal, or basically anything you want.

The best part is how much it changes the feel of your setup. A good wallpaper can make your whole PC feel more personal. You can also create your own wallpapers, adjust settings, and use it across different monitor setups, which makes it useful for almost anyone.

It is simple to use, has a massive Workshop community, and gives you a lot of value for the price. Once you start using it, a normal static desktop feels boring.

I would recommend Wallpaper Engine to every PC user, especially gamers, content creators, setup builders, and anyone who likes customizing their desktop.

I played this game for a few hours after I bought it and I didn't enjoy my time. After coming back to it a few months later I rediscovered a hidden gem in my library and I can confidently say that Wallpaper Engine has earned it's place as the best game I've ever personally played.

The game play in Wallpaper Engine is unlike anything that I've ever played. You can feel like the most powerful wallpaper creator in the game while still knowing that you are only one mistake away from making a bad wallpaper. The game is difficult in an unfair way that can initially be very frustrating as it feels that you have no control over your skill. One of the best parts of this game is it's cool designs. Due to the fact that everything is made by others and maybe yourself, it really makes you feel like you're just an observer in a real ecosystem. The people in this game is really talented and helps the feeling of an actual ecosystem with both creators and people who just use the wallpapers.

You play this game as a creature known as a person. The best feeling in this game is the feel of getting stronger, not from in-game upgrades or weapons, but from your skill increasing and learning new mechanics and techniques with the tools available to you. The movement in this game is highly skilled, which leads to the movement sometimes feeling unnatural or clunky, but then as you learn new movement techniques the movement  to click on stuff becomes far more fluid and natural, which leads to some of the best feeling movements in a game that I've ever played.

There's so much more to this game that I haven't covered here, but I'll leave that up to you to decide if you would like to try the game and become more familiar with it. This game is truly one-of-a-kind and itself is an experience. It certainly isn't for everyone but it is so unique and enjoyable that if you can make it past the first few hours of game play and get the game to "click" with you, then you may just find one of the best gaming experiences that you've ever had.

Wallpaper Engine is an amazing customization software that brings your desktop to life with animated and interactive wallpapers.
It is well-optimized and runs smoothly even on lower-end PCs, making it a favorite for gamers and aesthetic desktop setups.
